what_input_feature: The DeviceClass field at input offsets 0x0c-0x0f must hold the exact FOURCC 'scnr' (bytes 73 63 6e 72 = cmsSigInputClass). This is read into Icc->DeviceClass and switched on in validDeviceClass; only the 'scnr' value lands on the True side of case cmsSigInputClass at line 749.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed carries 73 63 6e 72 ('scnr') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, exactly matching cmsSigInputClass, so the switch falls into the case at line 749 (W-only) and returns TRUE.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 6d 6e 74 72 ('mntr', cmsSigDisplayClass) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, which dispatches to case cmsSigDisplayClass at line 750 (L hits T=10) and never reaches the cmsSigInputClass True side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer CMP on the 4-byte DeviceClass and writes a CMP_MAP bucket keyed by operand-distance; an input whose DeviceClass is Hamming-closer to 'scnr' than any prior input adds a new bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that guides mutation from 'mntr' toward 'scnr'. naive has no CMP introspection and must hit the exact 4-byte literal by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: The TagCount value parsed by _cmsReadUInt32Number at line 826 (a 4-byte big-endian integer in the tag-directory region of the ICC profile) must be numerically larger than MAX_TABLE_TAG so the comparison at line 827 takes its TRUE side and reaches the 'Too many tags' error path.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials (W shows T=9 F=0 at line 827) and reaches the W-only error block at lines 829-831; the CMP at line 827 gives value_profile an operand-distance gradient that drives TagCount upward until it crosses MAX_TABLE_TAG.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks in all 10 trials (L shows T=0 F=10 at line 827) and instead falls into the tag-reading loop (L-only lines 835-885 with L=T=181 iterations at line 836), meaning its mutated TagCount values stayed at or below MAX_TABLE_TAG; pure havoc never landed a large-enough value.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer comparison at line 827 and writes an operand-distance bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose TagCount is numerically closer to crossing MAX_TABLE_TAG adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, providing a gradient that naive's edge-only feedback lacks, so value_profile climbs to TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG while naive cannot.

what_input_feature: Bytes spelling the literal '<![CDATA[' opener (matched by CMP9 in the caller and CMP6 inside xmlParseCDSect), followed by content where the first char r is a valid XML char but the second lookahead char s is NOT a valid char (truncated/short CDATA section ending in EOF or an invalid byte right after one content character).
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds carry a '<![CDATA[' token (e.g. Seed 2 has bytes '<![CDATA[' at offset 0x33) so the parser enters xmlParseCDSect and reaches the s = CUR_CHAR(sl) read; the section is short enough that s evaluates to an invalid char, driving the !IS_CHAR(s) TRUE branch (W marks lines 9891-9893). The CMP_MAP distance feedback over the CMP6/CMP9 magic-byte comparisons gives the winner incremental credit for getting closer to the 'CDATA[' literal, so partial matches are preserved as corpus and refined into the full opener.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds either never assemble the full '<![CDATA[' opener (their early bytes are 'xlink:type', 'http:/xml', '!DOCTYPE' fragments, not the CDATA literal) or, when they do enter the section, they supply enough valid content that s is a valid char and the FALSE branch into the copy loop is taken instead (L marks lines 9894-9941 and the d=1 blocker row shows L T=0 F=8, never reaching the TRUE side). Without CMP_MAP distance feedback naive gets no gradient toward the multi-byte 'CDATA[' literal and rarely lands the precise truncated-after-one-char shape.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records the operand-distance bucket for the CMP6('C','D','A','T','A','[') and CMP9 multi-byte comparisons gating CDATA entry; an input whose bytes are closer to matching the 'CDATA[' literal opens a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving value_profile a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient toward assembling the opener that naive (edge-counter only) lacks.

source_line: case OP_ShiftRight: {           /* same as TK_RSHIFT, in1, in2, out3 */
what_input_feature: Input bytes that the SQL tokenizer accepts as a valid expression containing a bit-shift operator ('>>' = 0x3e 0x3e, or '<<'), so the code generator emits an OP_ShiftRight/OP_ShiftLeft opcode that the VDBE then dispatches via the shared 'case OP_ShiftRight' at sqlite3.c:86941. Reaching it requires a SELECT-like prefix plus a two-operand shift sub-expression.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are well-formed SELECT statements carrying shift/comparison operator bytes: Seed 1 'SELECT 1*9988878...>9=...' and Seed 3 'sELECT' followed by repeated '>>' (0x3e 0x3e) runs. The BYTE DIFF shows the winner concentrates the readable prefix S/E/L/E/C/T and '>'(0x3e) bytes at the operand offsets, and OP_ShiftRight is hit T=6 on the winner side.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds are short, syntactically broken byte soup dominated by ';'(0x3b), '+'(0x2b) and high bytes (e.g. Seed 1 'i;;;;;;; . Z;ELE', Seed 2 '#1;.. S..#$'); they never form a parseable shift expression, so the parser/codegen never emits the opcode and OP_ShiftRight stays T=0 (blocked) for the loser.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted CMPs (the tokenizer/keyword matching and operator-character comparisons) get progressively closer to matching valid SQL keyword/operator literals; the distance-bucket signal preserves partial-prefix SELECT/shift-operator inputs that naive's plain edge feedback cannot ratchet toward, letting value_profile assemble a parseable '>>' expression.

what_input_feature: Input must be syntactically valid SQL (a SELECT statement) whose expression contains relational/arithmetic comparison operators (>=, <=, =, -, /) so that sqlite3GetToken classifies a character into the token class whose true branch at 159496 fires and the parser proceeds into expression evaluation.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed is clean SQL 'SELECT 11-013>=1...' packed with comparison/arithmetic operators (>=, <=, =, -, /); these operands feed integer-CMP callbacks whose CMP_MAP distance buckets give value_profile a coverage gradient toward valid token-producing inputs, so it resolves 9/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: naive's loser-blocking seeds are malformed byte soup (e.g. '<.      ;.I/;;;.' and runs of 0c/cb/df bytes) lacking a coherent SELECT/operator structure; with only edge-counter feedback and no CMP gradient, naive blocks 8/10 and reaches the branch true side far less (avg hitcount winner=3 vs loser=31 on the false side).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts integer CMPs and writes a (PC, operand-distance) bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose comparison operands are closer to matching adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, giving a similarity gradient that steers toward the structured SQL+operator inputs that drive the GetToken true branch. naive has no such CMP feedback. Mechanism = CMP_MAP gradient.

what_input_feature: A well-formed SQL comparison expression whose operator parses to the TK_GE token (the two-character '>=' sequence) inside an expression that reaches resolveExprStep, so the switch( pExpr->op ) dispatches to the case TK_GE arm at line 98664.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT 11-013>=1 3=13...' — a dense, valid SQL expression that literally contains the '>=' (0x3e 0x3d) operator at offsets 0x0d-0x0e and is otherwise composed of digits, '-', '=', '<=', '/' that form parseable comparison expressions; the per-role overlay marks line 98664 [B]/[W] and DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows the winner takes case TK_GE T=1.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds contain comparison operators only sparsely and malformed ('SELECT ( 111111,...', 'SELECT+SE+...', 'SELECT ~~~~~44<4...'): they produce TK_LT ('<') or row-value parens/commas but never assemble a valid '>=' expression, so DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows the loser takes case TK_GE T=0 F=593 (never).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's integer-CMP interception writes a CMP_MAP bucket keyed by operand-distance: as the SQL tokenizer/parser compares input characters against operator-token codepoints and the parser state machine compares pExpr->op against TK_GE, value_profile's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to forming the '>=' token in a comparison context, giving a gradient that naive's plain edge-counter feedback cannot follow toward the rare TK_GE dispatch.

what_input_feature: A relational-operator character ('>' or '<') in sqlite3GetToken's lexer stream followed immediately by '=', so the true branch at 159511 fires (the second-character check that extends a single-char operator into the two-char form >= / <=).
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds carry SQL with adjacent '>'+'=' pairs: seed 0fadca0bde089a74 is literal `SELECT 11-013>=13=13...` containing `>=` and `<=`, and seeds c30df6313b55abb4 / ee8e63139c7a9d2c begin with bytes 3e 3d ('>='). The branch true side at L159511 is taken in W (T=3 F=0) but never in L (T=0 F=12).
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds put '>' next to ';' rather than '=' (e.g. byte runs `3e 3b 3b 3e 3b 3b` = `>;;>;;`, and the `vacuum` seed has no operator at all), so the second-char-is-'=' test never succeeds and the true branch stays blocked across all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: the tokenizer's character comparison against '=' is intercepted, and an input whose byte is operand-closer to '=' than any seen before adds a new CMP_MAP distance bucket and is preserved, climbing the gradient until the '=' lands right after '>'/'<'. naive has only edge-hit feedback and no such operand-distance signal, so it cannot steer toward the exact adjacent '=' byte.
